inference_steps: [0s to 1s] The opening text display is grounded as "TURTLE CANYON COMEDY PRESENTS" by both the sampled-frame summary and OCR on the early frames, and it is classified as an opening production logo rather than a subsection heading, so it must be excluded from the subsection count.

[3s to 4s; 65s to 67s] The sampled non-opening frames at 3-4s and 65-67s show action scenes with no visible title text, so these sampled visuals do not themselves visually confirm any subsection title cards.

[60s to 66s; 67s to 72s; 137s to 141s; 188s to 201s] For whole-video coverage, the preprocess summary identifies the video structure after the excluded opening logo: title card "THE DRINKS" around 60-66s, title card "THE BILL" around 67-72s, title card "THE FART" around 137-141s, and title card "THE PREP" around 188-201s. These are the subsection titles explicitly tied to distinct video spans in the supplied evidence.

[300s to 337.92s] The ending is separately identified as end credits from about 300s onward, and credits are excluded from the subsection count, so the supplied whole-video structure does not add another subsection title at the end.

Although the text context also names "THE INTERROGATION," the refinement instructions require counting only titles that are visually confirmed as actual on-screen subsection cards. In the supplied round evidence, there is no OCR-confirmed frame or other direct visual confirmation for "THE INTERROGATION," and no separate timestamped segment entry for it. Therefore it cannot be added to the verified count.

So the verified subsection-title set supported by the supplied evidence is: THE DRINKS, THE BILL, THE FART, and THE PREP. The excluded text displays are the opening production logo and the ending credits. Counting only the verified subsection titles gives 4, which does not match any answer option.

evidence.evidence_text: Retrieved candidate frames at 0.00s, 1.00s, 2.00s, 3.00s, 4.00s, 65.00s, 66.00s, and 67.00s for visual verification.

OCR on sampled frames confirms only the opening logo text in the early frames: frame 0.00s contains "TURTLE", "CANYON", "COMEDY", "PRESENTS" and frame 1.00s contains the same words. No OCR-confirmed subsection-title text is provided for later sampled frames in this round.

Whole-video preprocess summary describes the video structure: seg_001 is the opening production logo 'Turtle Canyon Comedy' / 'PRESENTS' and is excluded; seg_002 mentions title card 'THE DRINKS' around 60-66s; seg_003 mentions title card 'THE BILL' around 67-72s; seg_004 mentions title card 'THE FART' around 137-141s; seg_005 mentions title card 'THE PREP' around 188-201s; seg_006 mentions end credits from about 300s onward and those credits are excluded. The same summary also counts 'THE INTERROGATION' among the subsection titles, but no separate visually confirmed frame/OCR evidence for that title is supplied here.

Sampled frames show the opening logo in Images 1-2, hands/candies with no visible title text in Images 3-5, and a man with a menu with no visible title text in Images 6-8. The text context says to exclude the production logo/opening company card and notes the ending shows credits. It also presents the subsection names as possible on-screen titles rather than direct visual confirmation in these sampled frames.
